title: Melons vs Flowers
wikitext:
[]: Melons vs. Flowers             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Mar 06, 2008 5:55 pm I don't know why I never thought to post about this before, and, in fact, I can find no previous posts dealing with this subject: Why did Kaji posthumously tell Misato he was taking care of some flowers when they were, in fact, melons? I had initially thought it was a translation error, but it's still there in the Platinum version.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Mar 06, 2008 6:13 pm He didn't want to let on at what level he'd been thinking about her all this time.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Thu Mar 06, 2008 6:25 pm what ^he^ said

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Mar 06, 2008 7:42 pm Translation error? No. He did in fact say "hana". I'm assuming that means he was growing a few flowers along with the watermelons. It's a garden, after all.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Mar 06, 2008 7:46 pm "Flowers" represents virginity. "Shinji knows where they are" implies Shinji is a virgin. The request for Misato to "water them" means Kaji wants Misato to deal with Shinji's virginity, make him a man, i.e. deflower him.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Mar 06, 2008 7:57 pm BrikHaus wrote: "Flowers" represents virginity. "Shinji knows where they are" implies Shinji is a virgin. The request for Misato to "water them" means Kaji wants Misato to deal with Shinji's virginity, make him a man, i.e. deflower him.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Mar 06, 2008 8:37 pm Sachiel_13 wrote: BrikHaus wrote: "Flowers" represents virginity. "Shinji knows where they are" implies Shinji is a virgin. The request for Misato to "water them" means Kaji wants Misato to deal with Shinji's virginity, make him a man, i.e. deflower him.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Thu Mar 06, 2008 8:39 pm good GOD, I've watched that scene so many times and I can't believe I missed that Brik-kun is living up to his title (off topic: how do I get a customized title? )

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Thu Mar 06, 2008 8:54 pm BrikHaus wrote: "Flowers" represents virginity. "Shinji knows where they are" implies Shinji is a virgin. The request for Misato to "water them" means Kaji wants Misato to deal with Shinji's virginity, make him a man, i.e. deflower him.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Mar 06, 2008 8:57 pm @ Hal: It's an actual forum rank title, not customized.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Fri Mar 07, 2008 7:45 am Mr. Tines wrote: He didn't want to let on at what level he'd been thinking about her all this time.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Mar 07, 2008 8:08 am BrikHaus wrote: "Flowers" represents virginity. "Shinji knows where they are" implies Shinji is a virgin. The request for Misato to "water them" means Kaji wants Misato to deal with Shinji's virginity, make him a man, i.e. deflower him.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Mar 07, 2008 9:59 am I... don't see why growing melons is less "cool" than growing flowers. Then again, my innocent virgin mind probably just can't grasp the complexities of the metaphor involved.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Mar 07, 2008 10:07 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: BrikHaus wrote: "Flowers" represents virginity. "Shinji knows where they are" implies Shinji is a virgin. The request for Misato to "water them" means Kaji wants Misato to deal with Shinji's virginity, make him a man, i.e. deflower him.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Fri Mar 07, 2008 11:31 am BrikHaus wrote: "Flowers" represents virginity. "Shinji knows where they are" implies Shinji is a virgin. The request for Misato to "water them" means Kaji wants Misato to deal with Shinji's virginity, make him a man, i.e. deflower him.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Mar 07, 2008 1:16 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Epic fanwank WIN!

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Fri Mar 07, 2008 5:36 pm Brik-kun wrote: Does this mean I get to join the super secret fanwank club now?

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sat Mar 08, 2008 10:41 am NAveryW wrote: I... don't see why growing melons is less "cool" than growing flowers. Then again, my innocent virgin mind probably just can't grasp the complexities of the metaphor involved.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Mar 08, 2008 12:36 pm The only thing that just doesn't make sense to me at this point is the idea that growing melons is uncool compared to growing flowers.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Mar 08, 2008 1:21 pm Maybe "farming" is kinda a lame thing for a spy to do, whereas growing flowers is kinda of a luxury.
summary:
[]: Melons vs. Flowers             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Mar 06, 2008 5:55 pm I don't know why I never thought to post about this before, and, in fact, I can find no previous posts dealing with this subject: Why did Kaji posthumously tell Misato he was taking care of some flowers when they were, in fact, melons? I had initially thought it was a translation error, but it's still there in the Platinum version.
